rationale: FastAPI provides automatic OpenAPI docs, built-in Pydantic validation, and minimal boilerplate — ideal for a homework submission. In-memory storage via a plain Python dict keeps the implementation simple. The summary endpoint (Option A) is the most straightforward extra feature and pairs naturally with the balance endpoint already required.
notes: All data is stored in a module-level dict `transactions: dict[str, Transaction]` — no database, no persistence.
Pydantic models handle validation; custom validators enforce ACC-XXXXX format, positive amounts with max 2 decimal places, and ISO 4217 currency codes.
POST /transactions auto-generates a UUID id, sets timestamp to UTC now, and sets status to 'completed'.
GET /transactions supports query params: accountId (matches fromAccount OR toAccount), type, from (date), to (date).
GET /accounts/{accountId}/balance computes balance as sum of deposits/incoming transfers minus withdrawals/outgoing transfers for that account.
GET /accounts/{accountId}/summary returns total_deposits, total_withdrawals, transaction_count, and most_recent_transaction.
HTTP status codes: 201 for created transaction, 200 for successful GETs, 400 for validation errors, 404 for not-found.
requirements.txt pins fastapi and uvicorn[standard] only — no extra dependencies.
demo/run.sh starts uvicorn on port 8000; demo/sample-requests.http uses VS Code REST Client syntax covering all endpoints.
demo/sample-data.json contains a few representative transaction objects matching the Transaction model for reference.
